headline: Gold hotshot Forrestania buys Ramelius's Edna May for $300m

M0_1.event_statement: The Australian Financial Review reported on 28 June 2026 that Forrestania agreed to acquire the Edna May gold mine from Ramelius Resources for A$300 million. The Edna May mine, located near Westonia in Western Australia, had been on care and maintenance since April 2025 following the end of underground mining operations in May 2024. No additional on-list source has been located to independently confirm the headline facts of this transaction.
M0_1.primary_source: Australian Financial Review — Gold hotshot Forrestania buys Ramelius's Edna May for $300m, 2026-06-28
M0_1.source_grade_rationale: Australian Financial Review is on the approved sources list as a primary financial newspaper. The article is paywalled; WebFetch returned an access error and the facts could not be confirmed from the source itself.
M0_1.causal_language_review: Checked: 'acquires' and 'agreed to acquire' retained as observable transactional facts, not causal verbs. No use of: because, due to, driven by, as a result, leading to, signals, suggests, implies, panic, crisis, soaring, plunging, aggressively, inevitably.
M0_1.60_second_check_note: WebFetch of the primary AFR URL returned an access error (paywalled). Ten targeted searches — across Yahoo Finance, Australian Mining, Business News, MarketScreener, Stockhead, ASX announcement feeds, TipRanks, Kalkine, and the Ramelius / Forrestania investor-relations pages — found no independent on-list corroboration of the A$300m Edna May transaction as of 2026-06-28. Gate: FAIL → watchlist_no_source.

M0_2.gate_rationale: M0.2 was not run. Pipeline halted at M0.1 watchlist_no_source — no verified world changes can be asserted without an on-list source confirming the underlying transaction.
